what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for LIFTERs: deterministic code extractors that parse corpus units into produced_by:"code" projection values. Each extractor lives in its own lifters/<id>.py file declaring a LIFTER dict; the registry loads, validates, and indexes them.
observations: Registry loads modules from a directory via importlib.util instead of maintaining a hardcoded dict.
Every lifter file must declare a module-level LIFTER dict with id matching the filename.
Validation is fail-loud: bad id, unknown fields, or non-callable extract raises immediately.
produces defaults to the lifter id, allowing a lifter to target a different projection id.
as_records replaces the live extract callable with its __qualname__ for serialization.
rediscover clears the registry and reloads from disk so removed files unregister.
Files without a LIFTER dict are silently skipped; malformed LIFTER dicts raise.
standouts: File-per-entry discovery: adding an extractor means dropping a file, not editing a registry literal.
Strict id==filename rule makes each lifter addressable by its file name.
Mirrors the existing ProjectionRegistry/RoleRegistry/NodeRegistry mechanism rather than inventing a new one.
conventions: Follows the established registry pattern: os.listdir, importlib, id==filename, dict-like API, rediscover.
Uses a dataclass (Lifter) to wrap the raw declared dict with typed accessors.
Fail-loud validation for declared-but-malformed entries; silent skip only for non-lifter files.
Read-only extraction semantics: a lifter parses text, it does not resolve or dispatch.
concerns: for_projection scans all lifters on every lookup (O(n)); no inverse index from projection id to lifter.
as_records falls back to repr(fn) when __qualname__ is missing, which may be non-descriptive for lambdas.
register/discover overwrite existing entries without warning about duplicate ids.
Any import-time error in a lifter module propagates as a discovery failure; there is no per-file error isolation.
notes: A self-contained, file-discovered registry that turns executable extractors into validated, addressable rows.
questions: Which directories are passed to discover/rediscover at runtime?
How does the K2 corpus capture invoke for_projection and consume the returned projection values?
What is the expected schema of the value returned by extract for each projection id?
Is there a planned create_lifter authoring helper, and where will it live?
What content and format does lifters/AGENTS.md require for acceptance tests?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and implementation align: it is a file-discovered, self-registering, fail-loud lifter registry.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish code-side extractors as first-class, file-authorable registry entries that feed deterministic projections into the cognition corpus capture.
novelty: Distinctive concept of a LIFTER as a deterministic, model-free extractor that produces a produced_by:"code" projection, registered by dropping a file rather than editing code.
contribution: Provides a validated, file-discovered registry for deterministic extractors and a routing method that maps projection ids to their callable extractors, supporting a code/model projection split.
summary_for_embedding: A Python registry module that discovers and validates per-file LIFTER extractor declarations from a directory, exposing them through a dict-like container with routing and serialization helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To make deterministic code extractors first-class, file-authorable registry rows that produce produced_by:"code" projections for the cognition corpus capture.

symbols.does: Dataclass that wraps a validated lifter declaration.
symbols.description: Stores the lifter id and the raw spec dict, and exposes typed property accessors for extract, produces, and desc.

symbols.does: Returns the extractor callable from the spec dict.
symbols.description: Property accessor that returns the deterministic parse function declared in the lifter module.

symbols.does: Returns the projection id this lifter feeds.
symbols.description: Property accessor defaulting to the lifter id when no explicit produces field is declared.
Property accessor that surfaces the desc field from the lifter declaration, or None.

symbols.does: Loads a single lifter Python file as a module.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to import a lifters/*.py file and returns its stem name and module object.

symbols.does: Validates a declared LIFTER dict and builds a Lifter instance.
symbols.description: Fail-loud gate: checks that the declaration is a dict, id matches filename, only known fields are used, and extract is callable.

symbols.does: Container that discovers, stores, and queries lifter entries.
symbols.description: Dict-like registry that loads lifter modules from directories, validates them, and provides lookup by id or target projection id.
Creates the internal lifters map before discovery is run.

symbols.does: Loads all lifter modules from the given directories.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted directory listings, skips non-.py and underscore-prefixed files, imports each, and registers those declaring LIFTER.

symbols.does: Clears and reloads the registry from disk.
symbols.description: Removes all registered lifters and re-runs discover so deleted files no longer appear.

symbols.does: Validates and stores a lifter declaration under its file name.
symbols.description: Passes the declaration through _build_lifter and assigns the resulting Lifter to the internal map keyed by name.

symbols.does: Finds the lifter that produces a given projection id.
symbols.description: Scans registered lifters and returns the first one whose produces property equals pid, or None.

symbols.does: Serializes the registry as plain dicts.
symbols.description: Returns a sorted list of lifter specs with the extract callable replaced by its __qualname__ or repr for non-serializable surfaces.

symbols.does: Retrieves a lifter by id using bracket syntax.
symbols.description: Dict-like lookup that returns the Lifter instance for the given id.

symbols.does: Tests whether a lifter id is registered.
symbols.description: Dict-like membership check against the internal lifters map.
Dict-like iteration yielding the keys of the internal lifters map.

symbols.does: Returns the count of registered lifters.
symbols.description: Dict-like length operator reporting how many lifters are currently loaded.

symbols.does: Retrieves a lifter by id with an optional default.
symbols.description: Dict-like get that returns the default value if the id is not registered.
